title: Multi-Adapter Projection Requirements
goal: Implement #363 by defining the minimum adapter-target projection requirements that the generated command adapter contract must preserve for future non-Python surfaces.
non_goals: Generate npm, shell, PowerShell, binary, MCP, or skill adapters now.
Move runtime primitive behavior into generated metadata.
Reopen completed generation slices unless tests reveal a regression.

intent.outcome: The command adapter generation contract distinguishes universal command truth from adapter-specific rendering requirements and can plausibly project beyond Python CLI surfaces.
intent.constraints: Keep this requirements-only; preserve implementation-independent wording; do not add a new generated target.
intent.latitude: Choose compact field names and validation tests that make future adapter work reviewable without over-specifying implementation.
intent.escalation: Stop before target-specific runtime behavior, host-specific assumptions, or broad adapter generation.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue with #364 validated contract-consumption parity.
execution.proof: Run contract tooling checks, focused contract tests, generated conformance tests, planning surface checks, reconcile, and summary.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After multi-target projection requirements are recorded, validate contract consumption parity and decide the next generated adapter slice.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future adapter targets can use one requirements baseline without polluting core operation definitions with target-specific rendering detail.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Generating npm, shell, PowerShell, binary, MCP, or skill adapters.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: #364 remains the next dogfooding friction slice because contract consumption parity is still a double-entry check.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Contract requirements, schema, semantic checks, and focused tests passed.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Parent #356 still needs #364 and a later decision on the next generated adapter target.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Define multi-adapter projection requirements for generated command surfaces.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Requirements only; implementation-independent; no new generated adapter target.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: How to encode universal fields, target kinds, and adapter-specific rendering requirements in the existing contract.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The work requires generating or selecting target-specific toolchains.

immediate_next_action: Archive this slice and continue with #364.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
completion_criteria: The generation contract records universal command truth separately from target-specific rendering requirements.
Likely future target kinds have a requirements baseline without implementation commitments.
Contract tooling tests fail if projection requirements regress.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: The command adapter generation contract now defines universal command truth, adapter-specific rendering, future target kinds, and pollution guardrails for multi-adapter projection.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Contract tooling, schema validation, generated adapter freshness, focused contract/conformance tests, proof-selected workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, reconcile, and summary passed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No Memory or docs promotion; the durable baseline belongs in command_adapter_generation.json.

proof_report.proof achieved now: Projection requirements are schema-valid, semantically checked, covered by tests, and do not generate new adapter targets.

discard.summary: Detailed target wording iteration is not needed beyond the contract and tests.

continuation.summary: Continue with #364 to remove validated contract-consumption parity double-entry friction.
Keep parent #356 open for the generated adapter migration lane.
